evals.prompt: Generate comprehensive API documentation for this Express.js user management API. The codebase has CRUD endpoints for users at /api/users with GET (list + single), POST (create), PUT (update), and DELETE (remove). Each endpoint uses express-validator for input validation. Document all endpoints with parameters, request/response schemas, and examples. Output both a Markdown reference doc and an OpenAPI 3.0 JSON spec.
evals.expected_output: Complete API documentation covering all 4 HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) for the /api/users resource. Each endpoint should have a description, parameter tables, request body schemas for POST/PUT, response schemas with realistic examples, error responses, and curl examples. The OpenAPI JSON should be structurally valid with paths, schemas, and components.
assertions: Documentation includes all four HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE
Each endpoint has a human-readable description explaining what it does
Path parameters (e.g., :id) are documented with their type and constraints
Query parameters for the list endpoint (page, limit) are documented with defaults
POST and PUT endpoints have request body schemas with field types and validation rules
Each endpoint includes at least one response example with realistic JSON data
Error responses are documented with status codes (400, 404, 409, 422) and response bodies
An OpenAPI 3.0 JSON spec is produced with valid paths and component schemas

evals.prompt: Document this FastAPI application that has JWT authentication, rate limiting middleware, and paginated list endpoints. The API manages blog posts and comments. Auth is required for write operations but read endpoints are public. Rate limiting is 100 requests/minute for authenticated users and 20/minute for anonymous. Generate full API docs showing auth requirements, rate limits, and pagination details.
evals.expected_output: API documentation that clearly distinguishes between public and authenticated endpoints. Auth section explains JWT Bearer token usage. Each endpoint notes whether auth is required. Rate limiting is documented with limits for both authenticated and anonymous users. Paginated endpoints show pagination parameters (page, per_page, cursor) and response envelope format (items, total, next_cursor). Includes both Markdown and OpenAPI output.
assertions: Documentation includes an Authentication section explaining JWT Bearer token format
Each endpoint clearly indicates whether authentication is required or optional
Rate limiting is documented with specific limits (100/min authenticated, 20/min anonymous)
Paginated endpoints document pagination parameters (page, per_page or cursor) with defaults
Response schema for list endpoints shows the pagination envelope (items, total, has_more)
Public read endpoints (GET) are distinguished from authenticated write endpoints (POST, PUT, DELETE)
At least one curl example includes the Authorization header with a Bearer token
The OpenAPI spec includes a securitySchemes component defining the JWT bearer scheme

evals.prompt: Generate API documentation for this Node.js API that has extensive error handling with custom error classes (ValidationError, NotFoundError, ConflictError, RateLimitError), a centralized error middleware, and request validation using Zod schemas. The API manages products and inventory. Focus especially on documenting all error responses, validation rules, and the error response format.
evals.expected_output: API documentation with thorough error response coverage. Every endpoint lists all possible error status codes with their trigger conditions and response body format. The error response schema is documented showing the standard format (code, message, details). Validation rules from Zod schemas are reflected in parameter constraints. Custom error classes map to specific HTTP status codes. Includes both Markdown and OpenAPI output.
assertions: Documentation includes a dedicated Error Responses section explaining the standard error format
The standard error response schema is documented with fields: code, message, and details
Each endpoint documents all possible error status codes (400, 404, 409, 422, 429, 500)
Validation errors (400/422) include details about which fields failed and why
Custom error classes (NotFoundError, ConflictError) are mapped to their HTTP status codes
Zod schema validation rules are reflected in parameter constraints (min, max, regex, enum)
At least one error response example is shown per endpoint with realistic error data
The OpenAPI spec includes error response schemas in the components section
